    1. getMaximalWork(int, long)
    2. isGoalAllowed(G, int, long, long, int)

    5. getStopMessage(int, long, long, int, SingleRuleApplicationInfo)

Interface StopCondition<G extends @Nullable ProofGoal<G>>

All Known Subinterfaces:
IBreakpointStopCondition
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractCallStackBasedStopCondition, AppliedRuleStopCondition, BreakpointStopCondition, CompoundStopCondition, ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition, StepOverSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition, StepReturnSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition, SymbolicExecutionBreakpointStopCondition
public interface StopCondition<G extends @Nullable ProofGoal<G>>

Implementation of this interface can be used by implementations of ProverCore to determine if the strategy should stop or continue.

The first check is done before a rule is applied on a G via isGoalAllowed(ProofGoal,int,long,long,int). If this method returns false the strategy stops and the reason shown to the user is computed via getGoalNotAllowedMessage(ProofGoal,int,long,long,int).

The second check is after a rule was applied via shouldStop(int,long,long,int,SingleRuleApplicationInfo). If this method returns true the strategy stops and the reason shown to the user is computed via getStopMessage(int,long,long,int,SingleRuleApplicationInfo).

**Attention: ** It is possible that a StopCondition has to check one ProofGoal with the same underlying sequent multiple times. It is required that the called check method always returns the same result.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    getGoalNotAllowedMessage(G goal, int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied)
    Returns the reason why the previous check via isGoalAllowed(ProofGoal,int,long,long,int) has stopped the apply strategy.
    int
    getMaximalWork(int maxApplications, long timeout)
    Returns the maximal amount of work needed to complete the task, used to display a progress bar.
    @NonNull String
    getStopMessage(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, @Nullable SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
    Returns a human-readable message which explains why the previous shouldStop(int,long,long,int,SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.
    boolean
    isGoalAllowed(G goal, int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied)
    Checks if it is allowed to apply the next rule on the selected ProofGoal chosen by the GoalChooser before it is applied.
    boolean
    shouldStop(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, @Nullable SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
    Checks after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or if the strategy should stop.

  • Method Details

    • getMaximalWork

      int getMaximalWork(int maxApplications, long timeout)
      Returns the maximal amount of work needed to complete the task, used to display a progress bar. Pass 0 to indicate unknown size.
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      Returns:
      The maximal amount of work or 0 if it is unknown.

    • isGoalAllowed

      boolean isGoalAllowed(G goal, int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied)
      Checks if it is allowed to apply the next rule on the selected ProofGoal chosen by the GoalChooser before it is applied. If it is not allowed the apply strategy will stop.
      Parameters:
      goal - The current ProofGoal on which the next rule will be applied.
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      startTime -

      The timestamp when the proof search (apply strategy) has started, computed via

      invalid reference 
      #currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      Returns:
      true rule application is allowed, false rule application is not allowed so stop apply strategy

    • getGoalNotAllowedMessage

      String getGoalNotAllowedMessage(G goal, int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied)
      Returns the reason why the previous check via isGoalAllowed(ProofGoal,int,long,long,int) has stopped the apply strategy.
      Parameters:
      goal - The current ProofGoal on which the next rule will be applied.
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      startTime -

      The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via

      invalid reference 
      #currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      Returns:
      description of the reason why automatic proof search has stopped

    • shouldStop

      boolean shouldStop(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, @Nullable SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
      Checks after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or if the strategy should stop.
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      startTime -

      The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via

      invalid reference 
      #currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      singleRuleApplicationInfo - An optional SingleRuleApplicationInfo.
      Returns:
      true stop strategy, false continue strategy and apply next rule.

    • getStopMessage

      @NonNull String getStopMessage(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, @Nullable SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
      Returns a human-readable message which explains why the previous shouldStop(int,long,long,int,SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      startTime -

      The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via

      invalid reference 
      #currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      singleRuleApplicationInfo - An optional SingleRuleApplicationInfo.
      Returns:
      The human-readable message which explains the stop reason.
